data.title: MobiKwik IPO subscription status today: Issue booked 12x, retail leads with 43% subscription so far; check latest GMP
data.description:
MobiKwik's IPO got fully subscribed within an hour of opening of the issue on Wednesday, with investors expressing confidence in the strong growth prospects of India's thriving online payments market.

MobiKwik Systems initial public offering (IPO) continue to garner healthy response from retail investors on Day 2 of bidding as well, receiving 12.07 times subscription on December 12.

The maiden share sale by the fintech company One MobiKwik Systems received bids for 14.32 crore shares, as against 1.18 crore shares on offer, as per data available on the National Stock Exchange (NSE) at 1 PM.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The Retail Individual Investors (RIIs) part got subscribed 41.81 times, while non-institutional investors category received 16.33 times subscription, and the quota for Qualified Institutional Buyers (QIBs) received a mere 2 percent subscription.

This is the second attempt by the Gurugram-based firm for the public issue following its first attempt in July 2021. Earlier, the company shelved its IPO plans and withdrew the draft papers due to unfavourable market conditions.

IPO GMP Today: Vishal Mega Mart vs MobiKwik vs Sai Lifesciences

MobiKwik MD and CEO Bipin Preet Singh cited improved financial performance and strategic adjustments behind the IPO downsizing, reflecting a more realistic valuation, based on the current market conditions.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Inventurus Knowledge IPO: Issue subscribed 14% on day 1 so far; retail investors lead
data.description:
Inventurus Knowledge Solutions Limited (IKS Health) specializes in providing administrative and support services to healthcare enterprises.

The IPO of Rekha Rakesh Jhunjhunwala-backed Inventurus Knowledge Solutions had a muted start on December 12, with just 14 percent of the issue subscribed on the opening day. Stock exchange data revealed that investors bid for 14.4 lakh shares against the 1.03 crore shares available.

Retail investors were at the forefront, buying 50 percent of their allotted quota while non-institutional investors purchased 16 percent of their reserved portion. QIBs or qualified institutional buyers were yet to warm to the issue. Employee quota was booked 16 percent of the portion set aside for them.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

The Rs 2,498-crore public offer is a is a book-built issue, offering a total of 1.88 crore shares, entirely as an offer for sale. The allotment of shares for the IPO is expected to be finalised on December 17, with a tentative listing date on the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and National Stock Exchange (NSE) scheduled for December 19. The company will not receive any proceeds from the offer, with all proceeds (net of offer-related expenses) being directed to the selling shareholders.

Promoters of the company include Sachin Gupta, Rekha Jhunjhunwala, Aryaman Jhunjhunwala Discretionary Trust, Aryavir Jhunjhunwala Discretionary Trust, and Nishtha Jhunjhunwala Discretionary Trust.

Incorporated in 2006, Inventurus Knowledge Solutions Limited (IKS Health) specialises in providing administrative and support services to healthcare enterprises. The company assists doctors and healthcare providers by managing paperwork and administrative tasks, allowing them to focus on patient care.

IKS Health’s offerings include clinical support, medical documentation management, virtual medical scribing, and more. As a healthcare technology company, it aims to streamline processes, reduce administrative burdens for providers, and enhance access to care for patients.

data.title: Vishal Mega Mart IPO subscription status today: Issue booked 73%, NII quota full; check latest GMP
data.description:
Vishal Mega Mart's Rs 8,000-crore IPO will conclude on December 13, while the listing of shares is proposed to take place on NSE and BSE on December 18.

Vishal Mega Mart initial public offering (IPO) received 76 percent subscription on the second day of the share sale on December 12.

The maiden share sale by supermarket chain Vishal Mega Mart received bids for 57.75 crore shares against 75.67 crore shares on offer, as per NSE data at 12:15 PM.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The portion for non-institutional investors (NIIs) fetched 1.80 times subscription, while the category for retail individual investors (RIIs) got subscribed 74 percent.

The qualified institutional buyers (QIBs) part received a 3 percent subscription. Earlier, it mopped up Rs 2,400-crore from anchor investors.

Check All IPO News Here

The Rs 8,000-crore IPO of Vishal Mega Mart will end on December 13. Shares are available in the price range of Rs 74-78 apiece.

Shares are likely to be allotted to successful bidders on December 16, while listing is proposed to take place on both NSE and BSE on December 18 (Wednesday).

Vishal Mega Mart IPO GMP Today Price

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

According to market observers tracking the grey market premium activities, the shares of Vishal Mega Mart are commanding a GMP of nearly 30 percent in the unregulated market. Investgorgain quoted a GMP of Rs 21 for the shares of the company, indicating a listing gain of 26.92 percent.

Vishal Mega Mart's product range includes both in-house and third-party brands, covering three key categories -- apparel, general merchandise, and fast-moving consumer goods.

As of June 30, 2024, it operates 626 Vishal Mega Mart stores across India, along with a mobile app and website.

data.title: Sensex, Nifty decline ahead of India CPI data, weekly F&O expiry; energy, auto stocks drag
data.description:
Broader markets faced selling pressure, with BSE Midcap and Smallcap indices falling 0.6 percent and 1 percent, respectively, after outperforming in recent weeks.

Sensex and Nifty edged lower by noon on December 12, weighed down by the impending expiry of weekly derivatives contracts and anticipation of India's inflation and industrial output data due later in the day. Adani stocks were in focus with sharp gains, led by Adani Green Energy, which topped the pack and boosted the conglomerate's market capitalization by Rs 27,000 crore.

At 12:15 PM, the Sensex was down 213 points or 0.3 percent at 81,312, and the Nifty was down 81 points or 0.3 percent at 24,560. About 1,164 shares advanced, 2,214 shares declined, and 84 shares remained unchanged.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"The range-bound consolidation construct of the market is set to continue. The bull run in the US is continuing unabated with (tech-heavy) Nasdaq setting a new record and closing above 20,000 on December 11," said V K Vijayakumar, Chief Investment Strategist, Geojit Financial Services.

When it comes to sectoral performance, the Nifty IT index climbed over 1 percent, with all 10 constituents trading in the green. However, sectors like oil & gas, auto, and FMCG weighed on the market.

The IT index emerged as the standout performer, gaining over a percent, driven by in-line U.S. inflation data that bolstered expectations of a Federal Reserve rate cut next week. A potential Fed rate cut is a favorable development for Indian IT companies, which derive a substantial share of their revenue from the U.S.

Follow our live blog for all the market action

The Nifty Oil & Gas index dropped 0.5 percent, dragged down by declines in RIL, ONGC, and BPCL. Emkay Global noted in a November 17 report that the Indian City Gas Distribution (CGD) sector’s outlook has become murkier due to additional cuts in Administered Price Mechanism (APM) gas allocation by the government or GAIL.

The auto index slid 0.9 percent, dragged by Tata Motors, Maruti Suzuki, and Eicher Motors. The FMCG index declined 0.8 percent, with HUL, Tata Consumer, and ITC contributing to the losses.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Broader markets faced selling pressure, with BSE Midcap and Smallcap indices falling 0.6 percent and 1 percent, respectively, after outperforming in recent weeks.

"The mid-cap and small-cap space, which has shown significant momentum recently, now appears to be in the overbought zone," said Ruchit Jain, Vice President, Motilal Oswal Financial Services. "Adopting a 'buy on dips' strategy for mid-cap and small-cap indices might be more prudent at current levels," he added.

Also Read | CEA Anantha Nageswaran confident of 6.5-7% GDP growth in FY25

The U.S. Consumer Price Index (CPI) for November rose 0.3 percent month-on-month and 2.7 percent annually, both in line with expectations, according to the U.S. Labor Department's December 11 report. The CME FedWatch Tool now indicates a 99 percent probability of a 25-basis-point Fed rate cut during the December 17–18 FOMC meeting.

On the domestic front, the upcoming CPI data will be critical in determining short-term market direction.

"Technically, on daily charts, the (Nifty 50) index has formed a small candle formation, which suggests indecisiveness between the bulls and the bears," said Shrikant Chouhan, Head of Equity Research at Kotak Securities. He believes that the current market texture is non-directional and perhaps traders are waiting for a breakout in either direction. He added that a breakout above 24,700 could push the index to 24,800, while a dip below 24,500 might trigger further selling, potentially testing 24,400–24,350 levels.

Adani Enterprises, Adani Ports, IndusInd Bank, Tech Mahindra, and Infosys led the Nifty 50 gainers, rising 1–2 percent. On the downside, Hero MotoCorp, HUL, NTPC, Apollo Hospital, and Tata Consumer fell 1.6–1.8 percent.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Adani Green, Adani Power, other Adani stocks gain up to 7%; group m-cap jumps Rs 27,000 crore
data.description:
Adani Power, Adani Total Gas, NDTV and Adani Ports were also higher by 2-5 percent each.

  • Watchlist

  • Set Alert
 
 
live

  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Most Adani Group stocks traded with sharp gains on December 12, with Adani Green Energy shares leading the gains on the bourses, leading to a jump in conglomerate's m-cap by Rs 27,000 crore.

Adani Green Energy's  arm commissioned a 250 MW solar power project in Rajasthan. Following the commissioning of this plant, the firm’s total operational renewable generation capacity increased to 11,434 MW. As a result, shares of Adani Green Energy were quoting Rs 1,232 on the NSE, higher by 7.2 percent.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Adani Power, Adani Total Gas, NDTV and Adani Ports were also higher by 2-5 percent each. The sole laggard was Ambuja Cements, down around 0.3 percent at 11.50 am.

With the gains, Adani Group stocks added nearly Rs 27,000 crore to investors' wealth, thus pushing the group's market cap to around Rs 13.6 lakh crore.

Meanwhile, Mint newspaper reported, citing sources, that the conglomerate has initiated talks with a clutch of investors and banks to refinance $1.1 billion worth of loans of Adani Green Energy Ltd. The loans are up for repayment in March 2025.

(This is a developing story, and will be updated soon.)

data.title: Financial literacy will be key to India's "Vision for 2047": AMFI’s Venkat Chalasani
data.description:
Citing RBI data, Chalasani said that household savings are currently distributed as follows: 44% in real estate, 22% in gold, and only 34% in financial assets.

India's ambition to become a developed nation by 2047 hinges on improving financial literacy and expanding financial inclusion, according to a AMFI's Chief Executive Venkat Chalasani. Speaking at IFTA 2024, Chalasani noted that there is a need for transformation in the country's economic landscape, particularly in financial education and mutual fund growth.

"Our vision for 2047 is for India to become a developed nation. Financial literacy is crucial for this transformation," he said. Despite overall literacy levels of 77-78%, financial literacy in India remain low at just 23%. "Improving this allows people to make informed financial decisions," he added.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Citing data from the Reserve Bank of India, he noted that the distribution of household savings: 44% of savings currently go into real estate, 22% into gold, and only 34% into financial assets. "This distribution must shift if we aim for India to become a developed economy by 2047," he asserted.

Also Read | CEA Anantha Nageswaran confident of 6.5-7% GDP growth in FY25

Mutual funds have emerged as a critical driver of financial growth in the country. "Since 2017, campaigns under SEBI's guidance have educated people about financial products," he explained. As a result, assets under management in the mutual fund industry have surged from Rs 10 lakh crore in 2014 to Rs 68 lakh crore today. Additionally, Systematic Investment Plans (SIPs) attract Rs 25,000 crore monthly, with over 5 crore unique investors.

But he believes there is significant room for growth. "The global average for assets under management relative to GDP is 65%, while India stands at just 27%. Developed countries exceed 100%," he noted.

To address this gap, the industry is leveraging technology-driven innovations such as paperless investments and streamlined KYC processes. However, much more remains to be done. "We're working with universities to integrate financial literacy into curriculums, ensuring people understand goal-setting and informed investment decisions," he said. Expanding the distributor network is also a priority. "Currently, there are 2.5 lakh distributors, but we need 10 lakh to make significant strides."

Talking about trust, Chalasani noted,  "Comprehensive disclosures, stress tests, and risk assessments like the ‘risk-o-meter’ help build investor confidence," he said. Fintech firms play a pivotal role in this process by enabling better surveillance of investments and developing stress-testing models. "We’re in constant touch with fintechs to ensure transparency and mitigate risks," he added.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

data.title: Tech companies use Advantageclub.ai to boost retention rate for over 15 lakh employees
data.description:
Advantageclub.ai is now working on expanding its Generative AI offerings.

Tech companies are utilising the rewards platform Advantageclub.AI to increase employee retention rates of over 1.5 million employees. Deployed in IT and BPO industries, recognition has resulted in a 55 percent higher retention rate for recognized employees, and up to 78% higher retention when both managers and peers recognise them.

The San Francisco-based company, which counts Accenture, HCLTech, and Tech Mahindra as its clients, raised $4 million in a funding round led by existing investor Axilor Ventures on December 2, bringing its total funding to $11 million.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

“Recognition is very powerful and is driving business outcomes for tech companies,” Sourabh Deorah, chief executive and co-founder, of AdvantageClub.ai, told Moneycontrol.

Advantageclub.ai provides customizable white-labelled, no-code websites, enabling businesses to modify the platform to their specific needs. “That's how we have written the software, it's written in for no code, equivalent algorithms where we can configure different things using no code configs,” Deorah said.

The platform provides a range of reward options, including gifting, wellness programs, and long-service awards. Advantageclub.ai has managed redemptions totalling approximately Rs 1,000 crore annually, which is borne by the respective employer.

Deorah explained that five to six years back employees used to shift to rival companies, especially in Asia. However, that trend is now changing and horizontal shifts across categories have become mainstream.

“So everybody knows that they're not only competing with, say, other big companies in the same industry, but they also competing with other companies (to roles other than their core competency), the former Microsoft software developer said. “Everyone has up their game in today's world, I don't see any company which is not doing either rewards recognition… or fitness plan.”

Companies usually focus on retaining the top 60-70 percent of employees more, considering that talent layer as their Key Responsibility Area (KRA). Companies categorise and look at the retention rate for their high performers, average, and above performers.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

“Ultimately, they do want to ensure that their high performers and mid-level performers (stay back), and that's what recognition makes it happen,” Deorah added.

Adding AI

The company has doubled down on its AI strategy in the last few years, which is why it rebranded itself from AdvantageClub to Advantage.ai in July 2019.

The company introduced "Adva," an AI-powered recognition bot, that integrates AI services into its offerings. “We believe AI is an important part in the journey to really personalise the way people want to also drive engagement. And we have been using AI in some form, of them picking up the journey of the company,” he further said.

The company is now working on expanding its Generative AI offerings, which Deorah said is poised to become a cornerstone of its platform.

By building Applied AI use cases within the platform, the company aims to further enhance engagement and give new ways for organizations to engage with employees.

“We have taken a public LLM (large language model) and have trained it using the data that we have, and then personalise the query… so that recognition (looks) more personal and it stays fresher,” Deorah added.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: In Charts: 5 deviations in post pandemic market trends
data.description:
Margins, though improved compared to the pre-pandemic period, have declined year-on-year.

India's listed companies have experienced impressive growth since the pandemic, but there are signs that this trend may be slowing down, according to DSP Mutual Fund. Revenue growth (excluding financials), which had a 10 percent compound annual growth rate (CAGR) from FY19 to FY25, is now moderating to single-digit growth. Similarly, profits, which grew at a 13 percent CAGR between FY19 and FY25, saw only a 6 percent increase year-on-year in FY24.

Margins, though improved compared to the pre-pandemic period, have declined year-on-year. Excluding commodities, margins remained flat compared to FY24. Despite this, companies have strong operating cash flow and controlled capital expenditure, helping to strengthen their balance sheets and improve their net debt-to-equity ratio. However, the growth trajectory and valuations across various market segments suggest that the next three years could bring a different set of winners and losers.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

According to DSP’s report, sectors with low profit pools and market capitalization include Energy, Materials, Staples, and IT. On the other hand, sectors with high profit pools and market capitalization include Industrials, Financials, Consumer Discretionary, and Real Estate.

Sectors like NBFCs, Private Banks, and Oil & Gas have market cap growth lower than operating profit growth. Based on Bloomberg Consensus estimates, Real Estate is expected to see a higher operating profit CAGR for FY24-27 compared to FY19-24. Other sectors with expected higher operating profit CAGR for FY24-27 include Capital Goods, Metals, and Chemicals. However, sectors such as Telecom, Utilities, Automobiles, Retail, Healthcare, Banks, Cement, Technology, Oil & Gas, and Consumer are expected to see slower growth between FY24-27 compared to FY19-24.


Valuations across several sectors are trading at premiums to their long-term averages. Chemicals, for instance, have traded at a 50% premium to their 10-year average. On the other hand, financial sectors such as NBFCs and Private Banks have been trading at discounts, with NBFCs valued 15% below their 10-year average. Other sectors with the highest premiums to long-term averages include Utilities, Telecom, and Real Estate, while sectors trading at discounts to long-term averages include Energy, Insurance, and Private Banks.

The data shows that quality is exhibiting mean-reverting behaviour, as low-scoring companies in the bottom quartile are demonstrating performance patterns similar to those of high-quality companies. This follows a period of sharp underperformance of Q1 versus Q4 companies between October 2021 and April 2024.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad


Amid challenging market conditions both in India and globally, mid-caps are emerging as winners. Across metrics, data reveals that mid-caps are outperforming both large and small caps on parameters such as revenue growth, margins, ROE, and delta ROE. For example, revenue growth (CAGR for FY19-25) is around 10% for large and small caps, while it is 11% for mid-caps. Similarly, PAT growth for large caps is 14%, 12% for small caps, and 17% for mid-caps.


In terms of market cap distribution, large caps are currently lagging behind long-term averages compared to the mid and small-cap categories. The current market cap distribution of large caps is at 68%, compared to 72% on a 5-year average and 76% on a 10-year average. On the other hand, mid and small caps stand at 16%, compared to 14% on a 5-year average and 11% and 14% on a 10-year average, respectively.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Zaggle Prepaid lining Rs 950 crore QIP in two weeks to fund expansion
data.description:
The board of Zaggle has already secured an approval for a Rs 950 crore fund raise. This funding is for inorganic growth and strategic investments.

Shares of Zaggle Prepaid were sharply lower over 3% in early trade on December 12 after CNBC-TV18 reported that the share placement may take place in the next two weeks, at a discount, and the company may see an equity dilution of 15-16%.

The Qualified Institutional Placement (QIP) of shares is expected to happen at Rs 500-520 per share.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The board of Zaggle has already secured an approval for a Rs 950 crore fund raise. This funding is for inorganic growth and strategic investments.

"The whole idea is to be able to have this enabling provision with us so that we are ready to be able to tap into any opportunities that we come across from time to time, both for organic growth, international expansion as well as inorganic growth. Difficult to say at this moment how much would the split be, but broadly that is the commentary that we can give you," Avinash Godkhindi, MD and CEO, Zaggle Prepaid said during the September quarter earnings call.

The management is meeting investors and analysts at the Nirmal Bang Virtual IT Sector Conference on December 16.

The company has a market capitalisation of over Rs 6,700 crore and the shares are higher by 155% so far in 2024. Since its listing in September 2023, the shares have grown by 2-1/2 times. Track Zaggle's September quarter earnings here.

Earlier this week, the company announced an arrangement to offer HDFC credit cards bundled with Zaggle Software to its corporate customers across industries.

The company has been working on expanding internationally to leverage its success in the domestic market. The US market remains a key opportunity for Zaggle due to its vast potential, the management has said. "There are particular segments and segues which could possibly provide us an easier landing and faster expanding. We also see that there is a strong customer demand in the US for products like ours," said Raj Narayanam, founder and executive chairman of Zaggle, during the recent earnings call. One of its products Zaggle International Payments (ZIP) focuses on forex and remittances, which the company sees as a 'significant' opportunity to tap into a growing market of overseas travel for business as well as leisure.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The global spend management market is growing at a rate of 10.2% annually, and the Indian market is estimated to expand at a CAGR of 15.5%.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: India's most expensive security: PropShare Platina REIT dethrones Elcid, MRF with Rs 10.45 lakh price tag
data.description:
The company's Rs 353 crore issue was open for subscription from December 2 to December 4.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Move over Elcid Investments, there's a new sheriff in town! Following its debut on the bourses on December 10, PropShare Platina REIT has overtaken the holding firm to become the most expensive security available to trade.

In the previous session, the SM REIT settled at a whopping Rs 10.45 lakh per unit, after debuting at Rs 10.5 lakh per unit on the BSE on December 10.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

While PropShare Platina REIT has taken over the incumbent's spot as the most expensive security, Elcid Investments still remains the priciest stock. On October 29, holding company Elcid Investments shares surged to Rs 2.36 lakh crore, overtaking MRF as the most expensive stock available to trade. The stock surged 66,92,535 percent from a meagre price of Rs 3.53 per share.

The sudden upmove came after Asia's oldest stock exchange BSE conducted a special auction for the price discovery for investment holding companies. This auction did not have any price banks, in an attempt for a fair price discovery.

Elcid Investments is one of the promoter entities in paints major Asian Paints, holding a stake of 2.95 percent, which is valued roughly at Rs 8,000 crore.

Also Read | Elcid Investments stock, priced at Rs 2.36 lakh, could be worth Rs 5.84 lakh; here's why

PropShare Platina REIT is the first company to bag the  SM REIT license after the securities market regulator Securities and Exchange Board of India notified the SM REIT regulations in March 2024.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The company's Rs 353 crore issue was open for subscription from December 2 to December 4, which received strong interest and was subscribed 1.19 times.

PropShare Platina features 246,935 square feet of office space within Prestige Tech Platina, a LEED Gold-certified office building situated on Outer Ring Road (ORR) in Bangalore. Developed by the Prestige Group, the property is set to be fully leased to a US-based technology company under a new 9-year lease agreement.

The scheme offers investors a projected distribution yield of 9.0 percent for FY26, 8.7 percent for FY27, and 8.6 percent for FY28.
